source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
title: Ensure EBS volume encryption is enabled in all regions

input_row.source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure EBS volume encryption is enabled in all regions
input_row.description: Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2) supports encryption at rest when using the Elastic Block Store (EBS) service. While disabled by default, forcing encryption at EBS volume creation is supported.
input_row.rationale: Encrypting data at rest reduces the likelihood of unintentional exposure and can nullify the impact of disclosure if the encryption remains unbroken. Impact: Losing access to or removing the KMS key used by the EBS volumes will result in the inability to access the volumes.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Login to the AWS Management Console and open the Amazon EC2 console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/ec2/. 2. Under Settings, click EBS encryption. 3. Verify Always encrypt new EBS volumes displays Enabled. 4. Repeat for each region in use. Note: EBS volume encryption is configured per region. From Command Line: 1. Run the following command: aws --region <region> ec2 get-ebs-encryption-by-default 2. Verify that "EbsEncryptionByDefault": true is displayed. 3. Repeat for each region in use. Note: EBS volume encryption is configured per region.
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Login to the AWS Management Console and open the Amazon EC2 console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/ec2/. 2. Under Account attributes, click EBS encryption. 3. Click Manage. 4. Check the Enable box. 5. Click Update EBS encryption. 6. Repeat for each region in which EBS volume encryption is not enabled by default. Note: EBS volume encryption is configured per region. From Command Line: 1. Run the following command: aws --region <region> ec2 enable-ebs-encryption-by-default 2. Verify that "EbsEncryptionByDefault": true is displayed. 3. Repeat for each region in which EBS volume encryption is not enabled by default. Note: EBS volume encryption is configured per region. References: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/EBSEncryption.html 2. https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/new-opt-in-to-default-encryption-for-new-ebs- volumes/ Additional Information: Default EBS volume encryption only applies to newly created EBS volumes; existing EBS volumes are not converted automatically.
input_row.impact: Losing access to or removing the KMS key used by the EBS volumes will result in the inability to access the volumes.
input_row.references: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/EBSEncryption.html 2. https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/new-opt-in-to-default-encryption-for-new-ebs- volumes/ Additional Information: Default EBS volume encryption only applies to newly created EBS volumes; existing EBS volumes are not converted automatically.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control "Ensure EBS volume encryption is enabled in all regions" can be fully verified using AWS APIs. The audit section provides a specific AWS CLI command (`aws ec2 get-ebs-encryption-by-default`) that retrieves the configuration of EBS volume encryption by default for each region. This indicates that the property is exposed via AWS APIs, making it suitable for automated checks.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_ec2_region_ebs_encryption_by_default_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS CLI Command:** Use `aws --region <region> ec2 get-ebs-encryption-by-default` to check if "EbsEncryptionByDefault" is set to true.
- **boto3 SDK Method:** Utilize `boto3` with the method `ec2.describe_ebs_encryption_by_default()` to determine if EBS volume encryption is enabled by default across all regions.
- **Regions to Enumerate:** Enumerate all AWS regions using `boto3.client('ec2').describe_regions()`.
- **Configuration Property:** Validate the `EbsEncryptionByDefault` attribute in each region.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** Pass if "EbsEncryptionByDefault" is `true` in all regions; Fail if any region returns `false`.

This automated approach ensures that the audit process is comprehensive and consistent across all regions, validating compliance with the control requirements provided by the CIS benchmark.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control can be fully verified using AWS APIs, specifically through the AWS CLI and boto3 SDK, which are capable of checking the EBS encryption status across all regions. This makes the automated approach appropriate.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 9
- The automation details are technically accurate. The AWS CLI command `aws --region <region> ec2 get-ebs-encryption-by-default` and the boto3 method `ec2.describe_ebs_encryption_by_default()` are valid and correctly used to check the encryption status.
- The process of enumerating all AWS regions using `boto3.client('ec2').describe_regions()` is also correct.
- The program name `aws_ec2_region_ebs_encryption_by_default_enabled` is descriptive and follows the naming convention, though it could be slightly improved for clarity.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The program name could be improved to `aws_ec2_ebs_encryption_by_default_in_all_regions` to emphasize that the check is across all regions.
- Consider including a check for AWS Config rules that might already monitor this setting, which could complement the automation.
- Ensure that error handling is in place for regions where the API might not return expected results, such as newly launched regions or regions with specific restrictions.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-founded and technically sound, with a comprehensive approach to verifying EBS encryption by default across all regions.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the program name to `aws_ec2_ebs_encryption_by_default_in_all_regions` for improved clarity.
- Keep the automated approach as it effectively utilizes AWS API capabilities to ensure compliance with the control requirements.
